text.paragraph:
FNBR 37068902 Date(s): 11/11/20 - 11/11/20
Virginia Mason Issaquah 100 NE Gilman Blvd Issaquah, WA 98027- US (425) 557-8000
Discharge Disposition: *Home (01)
Attending Physician: Soong MD, Derrick L
Encounter Type: Clinic -Z Office Visit

tr.td:
1. Concern about growth
Reassured that he is following his current growth curve along the 10-15% line. Advised that we would not intervene or evaluate unless he is deviating from this growth curve. Discussed that interventions that can be done would be growth hormone but likely only if he was truly deficient. Advised we will continue to follow his growth annually.
2. Asthma
Discussed that if he continues to have cough in the night or during the day he would benefit from an ICS. Discussed that these can have some affect on growth potential as noted in some studies. Will do some research to see which ICS would have the least affect on growth potential and consider that. Advised parents to consider starting an ICS, they will think about it and get back to me.
Spent 25 minutes, greater than 50% was spent in face to face counseling regarding issues noted above.

text.paragraph:

Location: UV Video Visit
Appointment Type: Ped Behavioral Video Visit New
